behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module-to-client payload wiring, not model judgment. A test should run the classifier with a stubbed API client and assert the outbound request contains all configured labels. Rejected System because behavior text is about request content ("includes the complete list"), which is fully observable with in-process wiring; no real network/OS behavior is required. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no correctness depends on model output quality or multi-step agent chains.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier + taxonomy config + fake API client recorder; invoke classify(document); assert recorded request label list exactly matches taxonomy (no missing/extra labels).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at response-handling boundary: API output passes through taxonomy validation and public return mapping. Use a stub API returning an out-of-taxonomy label and verify module output is "unknown". Rejected System because behavior text ("API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy") can be reproduced exactly with a deterministic stubbed response; no real HTTP needed. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because this checks deterministic guardrail logic, not model quality or multi-step chains.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate classifier with taxonomy and fake API client that returns "medical"; call classify(); assert returned label is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets repeatability under repeated calls, and classification is delegated to an external AI API. A deterministic mock would likely always pass and hide real nondeterminism/drift from live model behavior. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-step agent pipeline where later steps depend on earlier model outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real API classifications for the same document under production settings; assert all returned labels are identical (or fail if variance appears). Track run metadata to diagnose nondeterminism.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: To catch real cost-control failures, test must observe actual token usage from the real external API path, because provider-side token accounting may differ from local estimates. Mock usage fields can hide tokenizer/accounting mismatches. Rejected Agentic because this is not about model judgment quality; it is deterministic API usage/accounting behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls for representative documents and read actual token usage reported by the provider/client instrumentation; assert each call is <= configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is retry orchestration across modules (error classification + backoff + retry + success handling). A controlled fake client can emit rate-limit then success, and a fake clock can verify waiting behavior without real network instability. Rejected System because behavior text focuses on logic ("waits and retries rather than returning an error") that can be reproduced with deterministic in-process error simulation.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake API client sequence: first call returns rate-limit error (e.g., 429), second returns valid label; use controllable clock/sleeper; assert one wait/backoff occurred, second attempt was made, and caller receives successful label.
